timeline.label: Q1 2026: consolidated gross profit +27% year over year; Cash App gross profit +38%; Adjusted EBITDA $1.0B
FY2025 10-K filed: revenue $11.5B (+10%); gross profit $10.4B; Adjusted EBITDA $3.5B (+14%); operating cash flow $2.58B
FY2025 share repurchases 2.5M shares; corporate bitcoin additions continued; bitcoin remeasurement loss $55.9M
FY2024 net income benefited from deferred tax asset release and bitcoin gain $420.9M; normalized earnings lower than GAAP
Ticker changed from SQ to XYZ on NYSE; company brand Block retained with Square and Cash App ecosystems
Afterpay acquisition closed; BNPL integrated into Cash App and Square seller tools
Corporate name changed from Square, Inc. to Block, Inc.; bitcoin treasury strategy formalized
COVID-era Cash App direct deposit and stimulus inflows accelerated consumer actives and engagement
IPO on NYSE as Square, Inc. (SQ); seller payments and small-business software core

industry.structure: U.S. merchant acquiring and SMB software is fragmented but consolidating around integrated platforms (Block, Toast, Fiserv, Adyen). Consumer fintech wallets compete with bank apps, PayPal/Venmo, and Apple Cash on engagement and monetization per active user.
industry.share_shift: Block is gaining gross profit mix toward Cash App (61% of segment gross profit FY2025) while Square GPV grows high-single to low-double digits; bitcoin trading revenue is flat but ex-bitcoin Cash App monetization is accelerating.
industry.trend: Regulatory scrutiny of consumer BNPL and crypto disclosures is rising; AI-assisted seller tools and banking features are the current product battleground; low-margin bitcoin revenue is being de-emphasized in favor of higher-margin financial services.
